kern/include/array.h

Go to the documentation of this file.

00013  * 2. Redistributions in binary form must reproduce the above copyright

00015  *    documentation and/or other materials provided with the distribution.

00018  * ``AS IS'' AND ANY EXPRESS OR IMPLIED WARRANTIES, INCLUDING, BUT NOT LIMITED
00019  * TO, THE IMPLIED WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY AND FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR
00020  * PURPOSE ARE DISCLAIMED.  IN NO EVENT SHALL THE FOUNDATION OR CONTRIBUTORS
00021  * BE LIABLE FOR ANY DIRECT, INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, EXEMPLARY, OR
00022  * CONSEQUENTIAL DAMAGES (INCLUDING, BUT NOT LIMITED TO, PROCUREMENT OF
00023  * SUBSTITUTE GOODS OR SERVICES; LOSS OF USE, DATA, OR PROFITS; OR BUSINESS
00024  * INTERRUPTION) HOWEVER CAUSED AND ON ANY THEORY OF LIABILITY, WHETHER IN
00025  * CONTRACT, STRICT LIABILITY, OR TORT (INCLUDING NEGLIGENCE OR OTHERWISE)
00026  * ARISING IN ANY WAY OUT OF THE USE OF THIS SOFTWARE, EVEN IF ADVISED OF THE
00027  * POSSIBILITY OF SUCH DAMAGE.
00028  */
00029 
00030 #ifndef _ARRAY_H_
00031 #define _ARRAY_H_
00032 
00033 #define ARRAYS_CHECKED
00034 
00035 #ifdef ARRAYS_CHECKED
00036 #define ARRAYASSERT KASSERT
00037 #else
00038 #define ARRAYASSERT(x) ((void)(x))
00039 #endif
00040 
00041 /*
00042  * Base array type (resizeable array of void pointers) and operations.
00043  *
00044  * create - allocate an array.
00045  * destroy - destroy an allocated array.
00046  * init - initialize an array in space externally allocated.
00047  * cleanup - clean up an array in space exteranlly allocated.
00048  * num - return number of elements in array.
00049  * get - return element no. INDEX.
00050  * set - set element no. INDEX to VAL.
00051  * setsize - change size to NUM elements; may fail and return error.
00052  * add - append VAL to end of array; return its index in INDEX_RET if
00053  *       INDEX_RET isn't null; may fail and return error.
00054  * remove - excise entry INDEX and slide following entries down to
00055  *       close the resulting gap.
00056  *
00057  * Note that expanding an array with setsize doesn't initialize the new
00058  * elements. (Usually the caller is about to store into them anyway.)
00059  */
00060 
00061 struct array {
00062         void **v;
00063         unsigned num, max;
00064 };
00065 
00066 struct array *array_create(void);
00067 void array_destroy(struct array *);
00068 void array_init(struct array *);
00069 void array_cleanup(struct array *);
00070 unsigned array_num(const struct array *);
00071 void *array_get(const struct array *, unsigned index);
00072 void array_set(const struct array *, unsigned index, void *val);
00073 int array_setsize(struct array *, unsigned num);
00074 int array_add(struct array *, void *val, unsigned *index_ret);
00075 void array_remove(struct array *, unsigned index);
00076 
00077 /*
00078  * Inlining for base operations
00079  */
00080 
00081 #ifndef ARRAYINLINE
00082 #define ARRAYINLINE INLINE
00083 #endif
00084 
00085 ARRAYINLINE unsigned
00086 array_num(const struct array *a)
00087 {
00088         return a->num;
00089 }
00090 
00091 ARRAYINLINE void *
00092 array_get(const struct array *a, unsigned index)
00093 {
00094         ARRAYASSERT(index < a->num);
00095         return a->v[index];
00096 }
00097 
00098 ARRAYINLINE void
00099 array_set(const struct array *a, unsigned index, void *val)
00100 {
00101         ARRAYASSERT(index < a->num);
00102         a->v[index] = val;
00103 }
00104 
00105 ARRAYINLINE int
00106 array_add(struct array *a, void *val, unsigned *index_ret)
00107 {
00108         unsigned index;
00109         int ret;
00110 
00111         index = a->num;
00112         ret = array_setsize(a, index+1);
00113         if (ret) {
00114                 return ret;
00115         }
00116         a->v[index] = val;
00117         if (index_ret != NULL) {
00118                 *index_ret = index;
00119         }
00120         return 0;
00121 }
00122 
00123 /*
00124  * Bits for declaring and defining typed arrays.
00125  *
00126  * Usage:
00127  *
00128  * DECLARRAY_BYTYPE(foo, bar) declares "struct foo", which is
00129  * an array of pointers to "bar", plus the operations on it.
00130  *
00131  * DECLARRAY(foo) is equivalent to DECLARRAY_BYTYPE(fooarray, struct foo).
00132  *
00133  * DEFARRAY_BYTYPE and DEFARRAY are the same as DECLARRAY except that
00134  * they define the operations, and both take an extra argument INLINE.
00135  * For C99 this should be INLINE in header files and empty in the
00136  * master source file, the same as the usage of ARRAYINLINE above and
00137  * in array.c.
00138  *
00139  * Example usage in e.g. item.h of some game:
00140  * 
00141  * DECLARRAY_BYTYPE(stringarray, char);
00142  * DECLARRAY(potion);
00143  * DECLARRAY(sword);
00144  *
00145  * #ifndef ITEMINLINE
00146  * #define ITEMINLINE INLINE
00147  * #endif
00148  *
00149  * DEFARRAY_BYTYPE(stringarray, char, ITEMINLINE);
00150  * DEFARRAY(potion, ITEMINLINE);
00151  * DEFARRAY(sword, ITEMINLINE);
00152  *
00153  * Then item.c would do "#define ITEMINLINE" before including item.h.
00154  *
00155  * This creates types "struct stringarray", "struct potionarray",
00156  * and "struct swordarray", with operations such as "swordarray_num".
00157  *
00158  * The operations on typed arrays are the same as the operations on
00159  * the base array, except typed.
00160  */
00161 
00162 #define DECLARRAY_BYTYPE(ARRAY, T) \
00163         struct ARRAY {                                          \
00164                 struct array arr;                               \
00165         };                                                      \
00166                                                                 \
00167         struct ARRAY *ARRAY##_create(void);                     \
00168         void ARRAY##_destroy(struct ARRAY *a);                  \
00169         void ARRAY##_init(struct ARRAY *a);                     \
00170         void ARRAY##_cleanup(struct ARRAY *a);                  \
00171         unsigned ARRAY##_num(const struct ARRAY *a);            \
00172         T *ARRAY##_get(const struct ARRAY *a, unsigned index);  \
00173         void ARRAY##_set(struct ARRAY *a, unsigned index, T *val); \
00174         int ARRAY##_setsize(struct ARRAY *a, unsigned num);     \
00175         int ARRAY##_add(struct ARRAY *a, T *val, unsigned *index_ret); \
00176         void ARRAY##_remove(struct ARRAY *a, unsigned index)
00177 
00178 #define DEFARRAY_BYTYPE(ARRAY, T, INLINE) \
00179         INLINE struct ARRAY *                                   \
00180         ARRAY##_create(void)                                    \
00181         {                                                       \
00182                 struct ARRAY *a = kmalloc(sizeof(*a));          \
00183                 if (a == NULL) {                                \
00184                         return NULL;                            \
00185                 }                                               \
00186                 array_init(&a->arr);                            \
00187                 return a;                                       \
00188         }                                                       \
00189                                                                 \
00190         INLINE void                                             \
00191         ARRAY##_destroy(struct ARRAY *a)                        \
00192         {                                                       \
00193                 array_cleanup(&a->arr);                         \
00194                 kfree(a);                                       \
00195         }                                                       \
00196                                                                 \
00197         INLINE void                                             \
00198         ARRAY##_init(struct ARRAY *a)                           \
00199         {                                                       \
00200                 array_init(&a->arr);                            \
00201         }                                                       \
00202                                                                 \
00203         INLINE void                                             \
00204         ARRAY##_cleanup(struct ARRAY *a)                        \
00205         {                                                       \
00206                 array_cleanup(&a->arr);                         \
00207         }                                                       \
00208                                                                 \
00209         INLINE unsigned                                         \
00210         ARRAY##_num(const struct ARRAY *a)                      \
00211         {                                                       \
00212                 return array_num(&a->arr);                      \
00213         }                                                       \
00214                                                                 \
00215         INLINE T *                                              \
00216         ARRAY##_get(const struct ARRAY *a, unsigned index)      \
00217         {                                                       \
00218                 return (T *)array_get(&a->arr, index);          \
00219         }                                                       \
00220                                                                 \
00221         INLINE void                                             \
00222         ARRAY##_set(struct ARRAY *a, unsigned index, T *val)    \
00223         {                                                       \
00224                 array_set(&a->arr, index, (void *)val);         \
00225         }                                                       \
00226                                                                 \
00227         INLINE int                                              \
00228         ARRAY##_setsize(struct ARRAY *a, unsigned num)          \
00229         {                                                       \
00230                 return array_setsize(&a->arr, num);             \
00231         }                                                       \
00232                                                                 \
00233         INLINE int                                              \
00234         ARRAY##_add(struct ARRAY *a, T *val, unsigned *index_ret) \
00235         {                                                       \
00236                 return array_add(&a->arr, (void *)val, index_ret); \
00237         }                                                       \
00238                                                                 \
00239         INLINE void                                             \
00240         ARRAY##_remove(struct ARRAY *a, unsigned index)         \
00241         {                                                       \
00242                 return array_remove(&a->arr, index);            \
00243         }
00244 
00245 #define DECLARRAY(T) DECLARRAY_BYTYPE(T##array, struct T)
00246 #define DEFARRAY(T, INLINE) DEFARRAY_BYTYPE(T##array, struct T, INLINE)
00247 
00248 /*
00249  * This is how you declare an array of strings; it works out as
00250  * an array of pointers to char.
00251  */
00252 DECLARRAY_BYTYPE(stringarray, char);
00253 DEFARRAY_BYTYPE(stringarray, char, ARRAYINLINE);
00254 
00255 
00256 #endif /* ARRAY_H */
Generated on Sun Jun 27 22:12:13 2010 for Os/161 by  1.6.1
